instructions: This is a modern NestJS + TypeScript API template running on Fastify with a modular feature architecture
Programming language is ENGLISH - variables, functions, classes, comments, and documentation
Exception: preserve business entities in original language when explicitly defined by user
Always use explicit TypeScript types and avoid 'any' (prefer 'unknown' + narrowing)
Use tabs for indentation (tabWidth: 4), not spaces
Use single quotes, trailing commas, and semicolons (prettier config)
Thin controllers, rich services: controllers handle HTTP, business logic lives in services
All DTOs are ZodDto classes; provide a schema name for OpenAPI: ZodDto(schema, 'Model')
Validate every input at the edge via ZodDto / ZodValidationPipe
Document every endpoint with OpenAPI in a colocated *.controller.docs.ts file
Apply @ApiKey() at the controller level; mark public endpoints with @AllowAnonymous()
Build custom guards with createSecurityGuard() from #libs/decorators
Include .ts extensions in relative imports and use path aliases (#libs/*, #testing)
Create comprehensive unit tests with .spec.ts files using Vitest
Add JSDoc comments for public classes and methods (main descriptions uppercase, params lowercase)
Use async/await instead of Promises; always handle the error path with NestJS HTTP exceptions
Follow kebab-case for file names, PascalCase for classes/types, camelCase for methods
Use '_' prefix for private/internal members
CRITICAL: NEVER hardcode URLs, API keys, or configuration in services or controllers
NEVER read process.env outside the dedicated config layer (src/app/config/)
Validate environment values with a Zod schema and provide config via NestJS dynamic providers (useFactory)
Non-secret config in env/appsettings.json, secrets in env/<env>.env.json (ships dev and release)
Mock injected dependencies with vitest-mock-extended (mock<T>()); no MSW in this template
Maintain at least 80% test coverage with meaningful, mutation-aware assertions
Use the NestJS Logger instead of console.log
Start features through the OpenSpec spec-driven loop (.ai/skills/spec-*.md): propose -> design -> tasks -> implement -> archive

context.httpClient: Built-in client via #libs/http (outbound); no ORM/database layer
context.formatting: Prettier with tabs, single quotes, trailing commas

development.description: Start dev server with hot reload (vite-node --watch)

testing.description_watch: Run tests in watch mode (for development)
testing.description_mutation: Run Stryker mutation testing for test quality

codeQuality.description: Check and fix code style issues, warnings and errors

controller.suffix: .controller.ts (thin; HTTP concerns only, delegate to services)
controller.security: @ApiKey() at class level; @AllowAnonymous() for public endpoints
controller.layout: Flat module layout under src/app/modules/<name>/ (no controllers/ subfolder)

service.role: Business logic, injected via NestJS DI (constructor injection)
service.config: Consume config through providers; never read process.env directly
service.errors: Throw NestJS HTTP exceptions and handle the error path

module.structure: Feature module per folder; register in src/app/app.module.ts

validation.schemas: Define Zod schemas with proper error messages and explicit types

configuration.principle: NEVER hardcode - inject config through NestJS dynamic providers
configuration.layer: process.env is read only in src/app/config/, validated by a Zod schema

testing.structure: arrange/act/assert with // shared variables, // mocks, // hooks, // tests sections
testing.mocking: Type-safe mocks via mock<T>() from vitest-mock-extended (no MSW)
testing.location: Place *.spec.ts alongside source; shared utilities behind #testing

jsdoc.main_description: Main description paragraphs start with uppercase
jsdoc.parameters_returns: Parameters, returns, and tags start with lowercase
jsdoc.example:
/**
 * Creates a user from the provided payload.
 *
 * @param dto - validated create-user dto
 * @returns the created user
 */

fileStructure.modules: src/app/modules/<name>/ - flat layout (controller, service, dto, docs, spec)
fileStructure.config: src/app/config/ - configuration layer (only place reading process.env)

avoid: Using 'any' type - always provide explicit TypeScript types
Hardcoded values - inject configuration through NestJS providers
Fat controllers - keep business logic in services
Skipping validation - every input passes through a ZodDto / ZodValidationPipe
Missing OpenAPI docs - document every endpoint in *.controller.docs.ts
Missing unit tests - create comprehensive .spec.ts files
Inconsistent file naming - use kebab-case for files, PascalCase for classes
Incorrect JSDoc capitalization - main descriptions uppercase, params lowercase
Spaces instead of tabs - use tab indentation
Double quotes - use single quotes consistently
Express-specific APIs - this template runs on Fastify
Non-English naming - use English for all technical naming
